title: Pork & Veggie Lo Mein
description: A quick and delicious one-pan meal featuring tender pork, colorful veggies, and savory noodles, perfect for a family dinner. This recipe is designed to be kid-friendly and easy to prepare.
instructions: Slice the pork loin into thin strips. In a small bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup soy sauce, 1 tbsp sesame oil, and 1 tbsp honey. Add the pork strips and marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Cook the lo mein noodles according to package directions. Drain and rinse with cold water to prevent sticking. Toss with a little sesame oil.
While noodles cook, finely chop garlic and ginger. Julienne carrots and bell pepper, and chop broccoli into small florets.
Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add marinated pork and cook until browned and cooked through. Remove pork from skillet and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp sesame oil to the skillet. Add carrots, broccoli, bell pepper, garlic, and ginger. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
In a small bowl, whisk together remaining 1/2 cup soy sauce, 1 tbsp honey, cornstarch, and water to create the sauce.
Return pork to the skillet with the vegetables. Add the cooked noodles and pour the sauce over everything. Toss well to combine and coat all ingredients.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats the noodles and vegetables.
Garnish with chopped green onions before serving.
tips: For picky eaters, finely grate the carrots and hide them in the sauce. You can also use smaller pasta shapes if lo mein noodles are too long.
Prep all your ingredients (chop veggies, slice pork, mix sauce) before you start cooking. This makes the stir-fry process much faster and smoother.
Adjust the amount of honey or soy sauce to your family's taste. For a little heat, add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the sauce.
